mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a robust labor market, supporting consumer spending and corporate earnings, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Strong labor market typically correlates with economic growth.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS growth is consistently positive but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of observations over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant acceleration in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) above a certain threshold signals rising input costs and potential central bank tightening, which erodes corporate profit margins and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Historical periods of high inflation often precede market downturns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when the CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth rate exceeds 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales (RSAFS) combined with stable or declining federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) indicates strong consumer demand without monetary tightening pressure, fostering an environment for positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending is a major component of GDP.
failure_condition: When RSAFS growth is strong and FEDFUNDS is stable/declining, S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 40% of cases over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-recession recovery, an increase in housing starts (HOUST) signals renewed confidence and investment in the housing sector, which has broad positive spillover effects on the economy and S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing sector recovery often leads broader economic recovery.
failure_condition: In identified post-recession recovery periods, if HOUST increases by more than 5% over 3 months, but 60-day forward returns are negative in over 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a deteriorating labor market and broader economic contraction, leading to a significant decline in corporate earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a short-term horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Spikes in unemployment often precede recessions and market declines.
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months, but S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 40% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates a healthy manufacturing and mining sector, reflecting strong supply-side capacity and demand, which supports corporate profits and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a key coincident economic indicator.
failure_condition: If INDPRO shows consistent positive growth, but the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of observations over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1) coupled with rising federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) indicates an overheating economy where monetary policy is tightening to curb inflation, potentially leading to a slowdown and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Aggressive rate hikes in response to strong growth can lead to recessions.
failure_condition: When GDPC1 growth is high and FEDFUNDS is rising significantly, if S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: In a low inflation environment, strong retail sales (RSAFS) indicate healthy consumer demand without the threat of monetary tightening, leading to sustained corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short-term horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending is a key driver of economic activity.
failure_condition: If RSAFS shows positive monthly growth in a low inflation regime, but S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in more than 45% of triggered instances.
